title: Meridian FinTech — Enterprise Security Risk Assessment
description: Apr 2026 – May 2026. Comprehensive security risk assessment and compliance framework for a simulated FinTech organization, covering threats, vulnerabilities, and mitigation strategies.
content: Conducted a comprehensive security risk assessment for Meridian FinTech Solutions, a simulated FinTech organization.
Identified security threats, vulnerabilities, and potential business impacts across organizational assets.
Developed structured risk mitigation and countermeasure strategies aligned with industry best practices.
Evaluated compliance requirements and provided recommendations to strengthen security governance.
Produced an enterprise-focused risk assessment and compliance framework report.

title: SijilChain — Blockchain Vehicle History Verification
description: Apr 2026 · Team of 3. Conceptual blockchain DApp to increase transparency in the used-car market by linking full vehicle histories to each VIN.
content: Designed a decentralized application concept aimed at reducing opacity and fraud in pre-owned vehicle sales through verifiable record keeping.
Reviewed registration and accident-reporting workflows to identify weaknesses in transparency and opportunities for data manipulation.
Defined the system architecture, stakeholder requirements, and a VIN-based model for tracing a vehicle's lifecycle events.
Proposed OTP-protected user access paired with immutable blockchain storage to preserve tamper-resistant vehicle history records.

title: Smart Vault — Secure Smart Postbox System
description: Apr 2025 – May 2025 · Team of 4. Smart delivery vault concept that protects packages through PIN and QR-based access control with mobile tracking.
content: Designed a connected postbox solution to safeguard deliveries using dual-factor access via personal PIN codes and scannable QR credentials.
Delivered an MVP covering mobile app user journeys and real-time visibility into delivery and pickup status.
Carried out market research and user validation interviews to sharpen the product direction and underlying business model.

title: Tesla IT Audit Plan & Audit Program
description: Nov 2025 – Dec 2025 · Team of 4. Risk-based IT audit plan evaluating Tesla's critical technology systems, cybersecurity controls, and high-priority operational domains.
content: Produced a risk-driven IT audit plan focused on assessing mission-critical technology environments and enterprise security safeguards at Tesla.
Conducted risk analysis and ranked audit priorities across vehicle software, AI systems, cloud infrastructure, and data privacy practices.
Drafted audit procedures and actionable recommendations to strengthen security posture, regulatory compliance, and operational resilience.

title: Cyber Attack Simulation Platform
description: Nov 2025 – Dec 2025 · Team of 3. Secure cyber range concept for running attack-and-defense exercises in a controlled, training-focused environment.
content: Architected a cyber range platform enabling realistic attack simulations and defensive response practice within a safe, isolated environment.
Defined system architecture, security requirements, and simulation workflows using SQUARE and STRIDE threat-modeling methodologies.
Built design models and Figma prototypes for user authentication, simulation management, and post-exercise reporting features.

title: Travel Agency Database Management System
description: Nov 2023 – Dec 2023 · Team of 4. Normalized relational database design for managing travel packages, bookings, flights, hotels, customers, and car rentals.
content: Designed a fully normalized relational schema to support travel packages, reservations, flights, hotels, customer records, and car rental operations.
Produced ER and EER diagrams and resolved connection traps to ensure accurate entity relationships and data integrity.
Implemented SQL table structures with primary and foreign keys, constraints, referential relationships, and representative sample datasets.

title: ShellSentry — Secure Natural Language to Bash
description: AI-powered web app that translates natural language into validated Bash commands and executes them on remote Linux hosts over SSH, with RAG grounding and layered security checks.
content: Built a full-stack platform that turns plain-English tasks into Bash commands using an OpenAI-compatible LLM.
Implemented authentication, password policy, and session handling for controlled educational use.
Added RAG with sentence-transformers and FAISS over a curated command knowledge base for safer generation.
Validated commands through whitelist, blacklist, and optional read-only policies before SSH execution via Paramiko.
Returned structured JSON results with human-readable summaries and optional LLM explanations of command output.

title: localhost — HTTP Server
description: HTTP/1.1 web server in Rust with non-blocking I/O, modular architecture, static file serving, uploads, CGI execution, and multi-port support.
content: Implemented a single-process, single-threaded HTTP server using epoll (Linux) or kqueue (macOS/BSD).
Organized the codebase into core, network, protocol, handlers, storage, and CGI execution modules.
Supported GET, POST, and DELETE with HTTP/1.1 compliance and non-blocking socket operations.
Handled static files, uploads, transfer encoding, sessions, and cookies.
Designed for learning low-level server architecture and event-driven networking.

title: Bomberman DOM
description: Browser-based multiplayer Bomberman clone for 2–4 players, built with a custom JavaScript mini-framework and WebSockets — no Canvas or game engines.
content: Developed a real-time multiplayer battle game maintaining 60 FPS with optimized DOM rendering.
Built a custom mini-framework without Canvas, WebGL, or external game engines.
Implemented randomized destructible maps, power-ups, safe spawn zones, and match countdown logic.
Added WebSocket-based real-time chat and player synchronization.
Focused on performance, WebSocket communication, and multiplayer game mechanics.

title: Ray Tracer (rt)
description: Photorealistic ray tracing renderer in Rust that generates PPM images with soft shadows, specular highlights, reflections, and gamma correction.
content: Implemented ray tracing that shoots rays from a virtual camera through each pixel into a 3D scene.
Calculated object intersections, surface normals, and realistic lighting per pixel.
Added soft shadows, specular highlights, reflections, and gamma-corrected PPM output.
Built for learning advanced rendering algorithms and 3D graphics fundamentals.
Structured as a professional-quality Rust graphics project with modular scene handling.

title: Smart Road
description: Real-time traffic simulation in Rust modeling vehicles, intersections, and road networks with live statistics and interactive visualization.
content: Built a Rust-based traffic simulation for modeling vehicles and road network behavior.
Implemented intersection logic and real-time movement of entities across the road system.
Tracked live statistics to analyze traffic flow and simulation performance.
Delivered an interactive visualization of road and vehicle dynamics.
Applied systems programming concepts for performance-oriented simulation software.

title: Maze Wars — Multiplayer FPS
description: Classic Maze Wars recreation with raycasting 3D graphics, UDP multiplayer for up to 64 players, mini-map, and progressive difficulty levels.
content: Recreated the classic Maze Wars first-person experience using raycasting 3D graphics in Rust.
Implemented UDP-based client-server multiplayer supporting up to 64 concurrent players.
Added a real-time mini-map, on-screen FPS counter, and live scoreboard rankings.
Designed three difficulty levels with progressively harder mazes and more dead ends.
Optimized rendering to maintain smooth frame rates above 50 FPS during gameplay.

title: Social Network Platform
description: Full-stack Facebook-like social network with profiles, posts, groups, events, real-time chat, and notifications — Vue 3 frontend with a Go backend.
content: Developed a full-stack social platform with user registration, JWT authentication, and rich profiles.
Built a Go REST API with 70+ endpoints plus WebSockets for chat, messaging, and live notifications.
Implemented posts with media support, followers, groups, invites, events, and privacy controls.
Containerized services with Docker and deployed behind Nginx.
Designed as a practical demonstration of scalable full-stack and real-time system architecture.

title: Wget — Command-Line Downloader
description: Full-featured GNU Wget–like downloader in Go with mirroring, parallel downloads, rate limiting, background jobs, and progress logging.
content: Reproduced core wget functionality for HTTP/HTTPS file retrieval from the command line.
Implemented background downloads, rate limiting, and parallel download support.
Added website mirroring with offline link conversion for local browsing.
Built progress display and logging aligned with 01-Edu audit requirements.
Structured as a modular Go CLI tool for networking and systems programming practice.

title: Push-Swap
description: Sorting algorithm challenge in Go using two stacks and a minimal instruction set, with a checker program to validate solutions.
content: Implemented push-swap to compute optimal instruction sequences that sort integers on stack A using stack B.
Built a separate checker binary to verify whether a given instruction list sorts the input correctly.
Applied stack operations and algorithm design to minimize the number of moves.
Organized the project with separate cmd/checker and cmd/pushswap entry points.
Focused on algorithm efficiency, edge-case handling, and clean Go project structure.

title: Net-Cat — TCP Chat Server
description: Multi-client TCP chat server in Go with nicknames, chat history, timestamps, and concurrent goroutine-based message broadcasting.
content: Built a TCP chat server allowing multiple clients to join a shared room and exchange messages.
Assigned unique client names on connect and replayed chat history for new participants.
Stamped each message with timestamps and enforced a configurable connection capacity limit.
Used goroutines and channels for safe concurrent client handling and broadcast delivery.
Implemented a netcat-inspired CLI experience for real-time terminal chat.

title: GraphQL Profile Dashboard
description: School profile UI powered by GraphQL queries against the Reboot01 API, with login, personal data sections, and statistics graphs.
content: Created a profile page that queries user data from the Reboot01 GraphQL engine endpoint.
Implemented authentication so users can sign in and access their own school records securely.
Displayed identification, XP, grades, audits, skills, and other platform metrics.
Built a mandatory statistics section with generated graphs from queried data.
Applied GraphQL query design and frontend data visualization in JavaScript.

title: 2048 — Twenty Forty-Eight
description: React Native (Expo) implementation of the classic 2048 puzzle with swipe controls, tile merging, animations, and live current/best score tracking.
content: Built a 4×4 grid game that spawns 2 and 4 tiles and supports swiping in four directions with smooth animated moves.
Merged matching tiles into higher values while updating the score and spawning new tiles after each valid move.
Ended gameplay when no legal moves remain and added restart plus persistent best-score tracking.
Used Expo with React Native for cross-platform mobile deployment on Android and iOS.
Applied mobile UX patterns including haptic feedback and responsive layout for different screen sizes.

title: Sky Map
description: Flutter app that renders a real-time sky map on a canvas using GPS, accelerometer, and magnetometer data to position celestial objects.
content: Displays the Sun, Moon, planets, and constellations on a live-updating star field rendered 10 times per second.
Uses the Provider pattern with geolocator for coordinates and sensors_plus for device orientation.
Loads celestial data from a local JSON asset and supports tap-to-inspect object descriptions.
Implemented position math and canvas rendering entirely in Dart for smooth mobile performance.
Built as a sensor-driven astronomy viewer for learning mobile graphics and hardware integration.

title: Chess — Multiplayer Mobile
description: Full-stack multiplayer chess app with a React Native (Expo) client and backend for real-time matches, move validation, and game sessions.
content: Delivered main menu, waiting room, and interactive game board screens for online and invite-based play.
Enforced standard chess rules with chess.js, blocking illegal moves and alternating white-first turns.
Implemented real-time multiplayer via Socket.IO with notifications when opponents move.
Handled terminal states (checkmate, stalemate, draw) with end-of-game messaging for both players.
Assigned unique session identifiers for waiting-room matchmaking and game lifecycle management.

title: StockSim — Stock Market Simulator
description: Flutter stock trading simulator with real-time prices, portfolio tracking, charts, and a wallet — built with BLoC, SQLite, and WebSockets.
content: Built StockSim, a mobile trading app for browsing stocks, viewing detail charts, and managing a virtual portfolio.
Used flutter_bloc for auth, stock, and portfolio state with a dark-themed Material UI.
Persisted user and market data locally with SQLite and shared preferences.
Integrated HTTP and WebSocket channels for live price updates and fl_chart for market visualization.
Implemented login, signup, wallet, and stock detail flows for a complete simulated trading experience.

title: Secure Messenger
description: Privacy-focused Flutter chat app with Firebase, E2EE secret chats, biometric unlock, group messaging, and FCM push notifications.
content: Developed direct, group, and end-to-end encrypted secret chats using RSA-OAEP key wrap and AES-GCM payloads.
Integrated Firebase Auth, Firestore, Storage, and Cloud Functions for messaging, media, and push delivery.
Added biometric unlock, username discovery, QR contact sharing, and friend request flows.
Tracked read receipts, typing indicators, and secure Firestore/Storage access rules.
Delivered a cross-platform mobile messenger focused on privacy and real-time communication.
